documents.title: Erie Canal Artifacts & Receipts
documents.summary: This exhibit case at the Brockport Community Museum explores the history of the Erie Canal and its impact on the town of Brockport, New York, from the early 19th century to the early 20th century. Key events and figures mentioned in the document include the opening of the canal in 1825, the major centers of farm implement manufacturing in Brockport, and the reconstruction of the canal in 1915. The exhibit also highlights the role of local businesses and individuals, such as the Samuel Johnston Co. and the Cleveland Construction Co.
documents.keywords: Brockport, Erie Canal, 19th century, 20th century, William Clifford

documents.title: BCM Destinations Overview
documents.summary: The Brockport Community Museum is a non-profit organization that brings local history to residents and visitors through interpretive panels and exhibit cases placed throughout the greater Brockport area. The museum, chartered in 2010, has no building of its own and is referred to as a "Museum without Walls." The museum's mission is to educate and engage the community with its local history.
documents.keywords: Brockport, Community Museum, Local History, Interpretive Panels, Exhibits

documents.title: Outdoor Art Interpretive Panel
documents.summary: The document appears to be a project plan for the Brockport Community Museum's Interpretive Panels Project, which was dedicated on November 14, 2020. The project includes the installation of interpretive panels, exhibit cases, and historic markers throughout the town of Brockport, NY, to provide information about the town's history and attractions. The project was funded by William G. Andrews.
documents.keywords: Brockport, NY, Interpretive Panels Project, William G. Andrews, Historic Markers, Exhibit Cases.

documents.title: Canal Commerce Panel
documents.summary: The Brockport Community Museum installed an interpretive panel on the Main Street bridge, funded by Express Mart and Northside Service Center, as part of a larger project. The panel was dedicated on October 29, 2011. The installation is located on the canal abutment railing on the south-east corner of the bridge.
documents.keywords: Brockport Community Museum, Express Mart, Northside Service Center, Main Street bridge, October 29 2011

documents.title: Historic Downtown Churches
documents.summary: The Brockport Community Museum installed an interpretive panel in Sagawa Park, funded by local churches and dedicated on October 19, 2013. The panel is located near several prominent churches in Brockport, NY. This panel is part of a larger project initiated by the Museum.
documents.keywords: Brockport Community Museum, Interpretive Panels, Brockport Churches, Sagawa Park, October 19, 2013

documents.title: Civil War History in Brockport
documents.summary: This document is an exhibit case from the Brockport Community Museum, highlighting the history of Brockport, NY during the American Civil War. The exhibit features the stories of two local soldiers, Morton A. Read and Lester Cady, who were awarded the Medal of Honor and imprisoned during the war. The exhibit also includes artifacts from the Erie Canal and its enlargement during the 20th century.
documents.keywords: Brockport, NY, American Civil War, Erie Canal, Morton A. Read, Lester Cady, Medal of Honor, Grand Army of the Republic

documents.title: Erie Canal Kiosk Project

documents.keywords: Brockport, NY, Interpretive Panels, Historic Markers, Exhibit Cases, Canal Commerce, Fire Fighting, Education, Downtown Brockport

documents.title: Early Farming & Farm Families
documents.summary: The Brockport Community Museum in Brockport, NY placed an interpretive panel, funded by the Town of Sweden Harvest Festival, in September 2014. The panel is located behind the Town of Sweden Farmers Museum and faces west towards Lake Road (Rt. 19). This is the eighth panel placed by the museum.
documents.keywords: Brockport Community Museum, Town of Sweden Harvest Festival, September 2014, Interpretive Panel, Lake Road

documents.title: Fire Department History Panel
documents.summary: The Brockport Community Museum installed an interpretive panel in 2011 with funding from the Brockport Fire Department. The panel is located in front of the Brockport Fire Department building on Market Street. The project was documented with photographs by Norm Frisch.
documents.keywords: Brockport, Community, Museum, Interpretive, Panels

documents.title: Firefighting in Brockport Exhibit
documents.summary: This historical document is from the Brockport Community Museum and showcases the history of firefighting in Brockport, NY, with a focus on the 19th and 20th centuries. The document includes exhibit case descriptions, photographs, and historical documents, such as a 1829 village ordinance requiring building owners to keep fire buckets. It also highlights notable events and individuals, including the 41st Annual Convention of the Western New York Association of Volunteer Firefighters in 1940.
documents.keywords: Brockport, NY, Firefighting, Western New York Association of Volunteer Firefighters, Capen Hose Company, Historical Documents

documents.title: Main & Market Street History
documents.summary: The Brockport Community Museum installed an interpretive panel, funded by 4th and 5th grade students, on May 4, 2013, in downtown Brockport, NY. The panel is located near the intersection of Main and Market Streets. The project is part of the Museum's efforts to engage with the community.

documents.title: Manufacturing in Brockport (Part 1)
documents.summary: This document details the proposed exhibit "Manufacturing in Brockport" at the Brockport Community Museum, featuring objects manufactured in Brockport, including items from various companies such as Karge & Son, Great Lakes Button Corp., and Owens-Illinois Glass Co. The exhibit aims to showcase the city's industrial history, particularly related to the Erie Canal and its enlargement in the early 20th century. The document includes a list of artifacts, their lenders, and the setup team.
documents.keywords: Brockport Community Museum, Manufacturing in Brockport, Erie Canal, Brockport NY, Industrial history

documents.title: Manufacturing in Brockport (Part 2)
documents.summary: The document is an exhibit case description from the Brockport Community Museum, highlighting the town's manufacturing history, including the production of pianos, glass bottles, and food products, as well as its involvement in the Erie Canal and its impact on the local economy. The document features various artifacts and notes the contributions of local businesses and individuals to the town's prosperity. The exhibit case is divided into three parts, showcasing the town's manufacturing history from the early 19th century to the mid-20th century.
documents.keywords: Brockport, NY, Erie Canal, Manufacturing, Industry, Local History

documents.title: Life of Mary Jane Holmes
documents.summary: This document describes the life and work of Mary Jane Holmes, a prominent American novelist in the 19th century who lived in Brockport, NY. The exhibit case features original copies of her books, showcasing her unique portrayal of strong, independent women. Holmes was a prolific author, publishing over 40 novels and short story collections, and her work was widely read by millions of women and girls.
documents.keywords: Mary Jane Holmes, Brockport Community Museum, 19th century novelist, Women's literature, Suffragette movement

documents.title: Updated Manufacturing Panels

documents.keywords: Brockport, NY, Interpretive Panels, History, Culture, Museum, Community, Education, Fire Fighting, Canal Commerce, Downtown Churches, Farming, Family, SUNY College at Brockport.

documents.title: A.D. Oliver School & Canal History
documents.summary: This document is an exhibit case from the Brockport Community Museum, showcasing the history of the Erie Canal and its impact on the town of Brockport, NY. The exhibit features various artifacts, maps, and documents from the 1820s to the 1950s, highlighting the canal's construction, operation, and eventual conversion to power craft. Key figures mentioned include William Clifford, District Superintendent of the NYS Canal Corporation, and companies such as the Samuel Johnston Co. and the Cleveland Construction Co.
documents.keywords: Brockport, NY, Erie Canal, Oliver School Building, William Clifford, Erie Canal Corporation

documents.title: Postcards of Brockport (1910)
documents.summary: This document is an exhibit case description from the Brockport Community Museum, featuring a collection of postcards from Brockport, NY, dating back to around 1910. The exhibit showcases various buildings, hotels, and landmarks from the time period, including schools, churches, and businesses. The document also encourages visitors to explore the museum's physical exhibits located throughout the community.

documents.title: The Protectives History Panel
documents.summary: The Brockport Community Museum installed an interpretive panel sponsored by The Protectives, Inc. on September 18, 1010. The panel is located in front of the Protectives building on West Avenue. The museum had previously installed two other panels.

documents.title: Public Education in Brockport
documents.summary: The Brockport Community Museum dedicated an interpretive panel on public education in Brockport, NY, on December 15, 2006. The panel was sponsored by the Brockport Central School District and featured area dignitaries, relatives of Fred W. Hill, and local citizens. The project was a collaboration between the museum and the school district, with contributions from various individuals and organizations.
documents.keywords: Brockport, NY, Interpretive Panels Project, Public Education, Fred W. Hill, Brockport Central School District, Local History, Community Museum.

documents.title: Retail Trade & 20th Century Commerce
documents.summary: This exhibit case showcases the retail trade and commerce in Brockport, NY, with items dating back to the mid-19th century. The display includes advertisements, trade cards, and other promotional items from businesses such as insurance agencies, confectionery shops, and shoe stores. The case highlights the evolution of retail in Brockport from the 1850s to the mid-20th century.
documents.keywords: Brockport, retail trade, commerce, Vick's Directory, 1907, advertising, trade cards.

documents.title: Sagawa Park Murals
documents.summary: The Brockport Community Museum's Interpretive Panels Project features a series of panels dedicated to the Sagawa Park mural, with one panel funded by the Fred Hill Elementary School 4th and 5th grade classes and dedicated on December 2, 2016. The project involved invitations and a program distributed at the panel dedication, which included guests such as local officials, school administrators, and museum representatives. The project aimed to interpret and showcase the mural in Sagawa Park.
